title: Simulation Experience Asset (SEA)
description: One unit of captured, machine-verifiable simulation experience. The atom of Org_system's Tier-1 experience layer. An SEA is only trustworthy while its `verification.last_verdict` is REPRODUCED and it is not `stale`. The verifier — not a human approval gate — is what promotes a candidate to verified and what retires it when it rots.

kind.description: Which invisible-asset type this is. Phase 0 implements only 'converged_config'; the rest are Phase 2.

captured_from.description: Run id / workflow id the asset was distilled from.

captured_by.description: Agent or process that captured it.

origin_actor.description: Human or agent whose work produced the original run (for who-knows-what / transactive memory).

source_refs.description: Papers, tickets, prior SEA ids that justify this asset (e.g. 'Du & Perré 2016').

consent.description: Scoped, inspectable capture permission. Surveillance-tension mitigation (spec §9).

scope.description: What the origin actor authorized to be captured from this run.

environment.description: Everything needed to reconstruct the execution environment. The 'incantations' from doc §3.

versions.description: Version/hash of every component that can change the result (jar hash, java version, os, key libs).

submission.description: Exact command / submission script that launches the run.

config_ref.description: Path/URI to the protocol/config artifact (e.g. postia_placenta_validation.xml).

config_hash.description: Content hash of the config — makes the asset content-addressed and tamper-evident.

data_refs.description: Input data hashes/URIs and their provenance/quirks (doc §3).

salient_parameters.description: The handful of parameters a human/agent should read to understand the asset.

expected_result.description: The machine-checkable claim this asset makes. Verification succeeds iff a re-run reproduces these within tolerance.

verification.description: How this asset is (re)checked. The reproducibility gate.

method.description: Phase 0 uses rerun_and_compare. rerun_expect_failure verifies negative_result assets. Later kinds map to later methods.

n_repeats.description: For stochastic runs: repeats to form a distribution the comparator tests against.

comparator.description: Named comparator/tolerance policy that turns a re-run into a verdict.

last_verdict.description: REPRODUCED is the only trustworthy state. DIVERGED/ENV_BROKEN/INCONCLUSIVE force status change.

reverify_after_days.description: Rot control: re-run this asset on a schedule; on failure move status -> stale/retired.

rationale.description: The 'why it works' / 'why we don't do X anymore'. The Polanyi target (doc §3) — may be thin or empty at capture; enriched over time. Never trusted without the verifier; it explains, it does not certify.

status.description: candidate = captured, not yet reproduced by a second run. verified = REPRODUCED. stale = failed a scheduled re-verify or its environment moved. retired = superseded or permanently non-reproducing.

links.description: Related SEA ids (supersedes, contradicts, derived-from). Lets negative results point at the config they rule out.
